items.title: Saints are fit for Colts nickel Kenny Moore II in search for new team
items.summary: The New Orleans Saints never did replace Alontae Taylor after losing him in free agency. Kenny Moore II would be a great fit after the Colts cut him.

Plus: Coordinators talk about the draft and offseason needs, Josh Downs discusses an [...]

items.title: What Longhorns QB Arch Manning needs to improve on to be No. 1 pick
items.summary: The maturation of Longhorns QB Arch Manning was evident in 2025. But if he wants to be No. 1 pick in 2027 NFL Draft, he has to improve in this area.

items.title: Colts release Kenny Moore II
items.summary: On Thursday afternoon, the Colts announced they released Kenny Moore II after being unable to find a trade partner for their longtime starting nickel corner following his pre-Draft trade request. Other moves announced on Thursday was the release of recently unreturned Safety Nasir Adderley and waiving linebacker John Bullock. Kenny Moore II was a fan […]

items.title: Colts part ways with Kenny Moore II
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Kenny Moore II’s career will resume elsewhere. One of the longest-tenured and most respected Indianapolis Colts was released on Thursday. Moore and general manager Chris Ballard recently met and decided the best path forward was a trade. When that failed to materialize, the decision was made to release Moore and allow him to [...]

items.title: Colts release CB Kenny Moore II, S Nasir Adderley; waive LB John Bullock
items.summary: Moore, who spent the last nine seasons with the Colts, requested a trade in April.

items.title: Poll Results: How good do you want the Colts to be this year?
items.summary: The short story is that 177 (71.66%) votes were cast that wanted the Colts to win, even if it meant retaining Ballard and Steichen. That left 70 (28.34%) voters who would seemingly prefer our current leadership to be gone, no matter this year’s results. 247 votes were cast, so the sample size is likely healthy […]

items.title: WR Josh Downs hopes for new opportunities in 2026
items.summary: With Michael Pittman Jr. no longer on the team due to a trade, the outside receiver spot is open.

items.title: How O-line coach Tony Sparano Jr. has become a team-building weapon for Colts
items.summary: The Colts are one of two NFL teams to have a completely home-grown starting offensive line, and all but one of those starters for Tony Sparano Jr.'s group was a top-75 draft pick.

items.title: Big brother Josh Downs rode the emotional wave (and wore the Dallas hat) after Cowboys drafted Caleb
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – For a few emotional hours, Josh Downs’ loyalties were family-driven. As the Indianapolis Colts’ veteran receiver, family and friends celebrated younger brother Caleb being drafted No. 11 overall by Dallas in the April 23 NFL draft, Josh Downs pulled on a Cowboys hat. “I was wearing that hat all night,’’ he said Wednesday [...]

items.title: Which Colts rookies have the best chance of starting this season?
items.summary: Likely Starter CJ Allen is the cleanest projection to start right away. The Colts’ linebacker room is thin, and Akeem Davis-Gaither is the only player with meaningful previous starting experience. Jaylon Carlies has not carved out a consistent role, and there isn’t another obvious player blocking Allen from taking a major role. Allen also fits […]

items.title: Richardson reporting to offseason workouts adds intrigue to the Colts’ QB room
items.summary: Well, well, well. Anthony Richardson ended his two-week holdout and is back in the building with the Indianapolis Colts for voluntary offseason workouts. While labeling it a “holdout” is cheeky, Richardson wasn’t overly keen to rejoin the team, but with no trade in sight and money and potential playing time on the line, he decided […]

I suggested that I believed that the negativity towards Chris Ballard had reached a point that many fans would trade a good season, for a bad one that produced his ouster. I was shocked that they decided to address the safety position with their limited resources in this year’s draft— but I really shouldn’t […]

items.title: Imagine if you had done what the Colts did
items.summary: In unsurprising news, the Indianapolis Colts declined Anthony Richardson’s fifth-year option. The former fourth overall pick is set to be an unrestricted free agent next year which marks the final nail in the coffin. With no trade suitors, the Colts are prepared to run out the clock. This is the reality of the situation. The […]

items.summary: Eric Moore, head football coach at Center Grove High School, spoke to Matt Taylor about coaching the Colts' sixth-round pick at CGHS.

items.title: Rookie paydays slotted for Colts’ draft picks
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The signings are trickling in, which soon will be replaced by a deluge. The days of protracted negotiations with draft picks are a distant memory. Using recent history as a blueprint, the Indianapolis Colts will have most, if not all, of their 2026 draft class under contract in the next week or two. [...]

items.title: No fifth-year option for Colts’ QB Anthony Richardson Sr.
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts won’t trigger the fifth-year option of quarterback Anthony Richardson, according to a source. And that isn’t a surprise. The deadline for teams to exercise the fifth-year option for 2023 first-round picks is Friday, and there will be no action from Indy. At the end of the NFL draft Saturday, general [...]

items.summary: The 2026 NFL Draft is in the books, and the Indianapolis Colts went a long way in addressing some of their top needs. Dave Griffiths, Mike Chappell and Matt Adams look at the eight players in this year's class and the impact they could have on the upcoming season.

items.title: Colts gave Seth McGowan second chance after 2021 arrest nearly cost him everything
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The incident was addressed every time Seth McGowan met with a prospective employer. That included when he shared time with the Indianapolis Colts. “We vetted hard,’’ general manager Chris Ballard said. McGowan’s persistent plea: I made a serious mistake, but I learned from it. You can trust me. Finally, McGowan’s long wait during [...]

items.title: Indianapolis Colts and Eleven launch program to help Hoosier small businesses connect & grow
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts have teamed up with Eleven Sports Media to launch a brand‑new Small Business Partner Program, creating an unprecedented opportunity for small and medium‑sized businesses across Indiana to connect, grow, and align with the team.

The draft added Zaire Franklin’s successor at middle linebacker [...]

items.summary: The Colts made eight selections in the 2026 NFL Draft, from CJ Allen in the second round to Deion Burks in the seventh round. Here's how each player could impact the Colts and the process it took for Chris Ballard to land them.

items.title: Post-draft ‘frenzy’ adds to Colts’ roster-building
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The storm builds as the NFL Draft winds down. The search is exhaustive to uncover the next Gary Brackett, Dominic Rhodes, Joe Reitz, Jack Doyle, Kenny Moore II and so many others who overcame the temporary setback of not being drafted to carve out a solid NFL career. Doyle and Moore have Pro [...]

items.title: A capsule look at the Colts’ 8 draft picks
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — The Indianapolis Colts injected eight draft picks into their roster, including two projected starters this week. Second-round pick CJ Allen, a linebacker out of Georgia, and third-rounder A.J. Haulcy, an LSU safety, bring immediate impact potential. That’s especially true with Allen, who’ll replaced Zaire Franklin and run the defense from the Mike position. [...]

items.title: No trades for Colts’ Anthony Richardson Sr., Kenny Moore II
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — Post-draft, it’s status quo with two prominent Indianapolis Colts. The three-day, seven-round, 257-pick NFL Draft ended Saturday evening without quarterback Anthony Richardson Sr. and cornerback Kenny Moore II being traded. That would have been an ideal time for the Colts to resolve trade requests two players submitted. In Richardson’s instance, no team was [...]

items.title: Colts’ Chris Ballard: We got younger, faster on defense
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The major renovation on defense picked up steam Friday night. After a trade-back with Pittsburgh in round 2 – No. 47 overall to No. 53 – the Indianapolis Colts found Zaire Franklin’s successor at middle linebacker with Georgia’s CJ Allen. In round 3, they invested the 78th overall pick in LSU safety A.J. Haulcy. [...]
